summary: Drop root-level auth/config/delivery flags from MCP structured arguments and reject flag-like tokens in the free-form args field.
reason: The generated MCP shellout bridge accepted every structured key as a CLI flag. That let an MCP caller override root control-plane flags such as --config, --token, --profile, and --deliver instead of staying within the per-command tool surface.
upstream_issue: cli-printing-press: generated MCP shellout code should include the root-flag blocklist and args-field guard by default.
